About BHM Services USA

Air Specialists

Service 01
Air Duct Cleaning
Professional Deep-Clean · NADCA Certified Methods

Your home's air ducts are the lungs of your house — every breath you take passes through them. Over time, ducts accumulate dust, mold spores, pet dander, pollen, bacteria, and even rodent droppings, all of which get recirculated through your living spaces every time the HVAC system runs.

At BHM Services USA, we use industrial-grade negative air pressure equipment combined with high-powered rotary brushes to dislodge and extract every particle from your ductwork — from the main trunk lines all the way to every supply and return register in your home.

Before & After — Duct Interior
Real Results — BHM Services USA
Why Air Duct Cleaning Matters
🫁
Healthier Air Quality

The EPA estimates indoor air can be 2–5x more polluted than outdoor air. Clean ducts dramatically reduce allergens, bacteria, and mold spores circulating in your home.

⚡
Lower Energy Bills

Clogged ducts force your HVAC to work harder, consuming up to 30% more energy. Clean ducts restore optimal airflow and can significantly reduce monthly utility costs.

🔧
Extends HVAC Life

Dust buildup on coils, fans, and motors causes premature wear and costly breakdowns. Regular cleaning extends the life of your entire HVAC system by years.

🏠
Less Dust in Your Home

If you're constantly dusting surfaces shortly after cleaning, your ducts are likely the culprit. Clean ducts mean a noticeably cleaner home with far less maintenance.

👶
Safer for Children & Elderly

Young children and elderly family members are especially vulnerable to airborne contaminants. Clean ducts provide a safer breathing environment for your most vulnerable loved ones.

🦠
Eliminates Mold & Bacteria

Humid conditions inside ductwork can breed mold colonies. Our process includes sanitization treatments that eliminate biological growth and prevent future buildup.

Signs You Need Air Duct Cleaning
Visible dust blowing from vents when HVAC runs
Allergy or asthma symptoms worsening indoors
Musty or stale odors when the system is on
Rapid dust accumulation on furniture
Uneven airflow between rooms
Recent renovation or construction work done
Never had your ducts cleaned in 3+ years
Rodent or insect activity detected in home
Our Process
1
Full System Inspection

We begin with a thorough camera inspection of your ductwork to assess the level of contamination, identify any damage or leaks, and create a cleaning plan tailored to your home.

2
Negative Air Pressure Setup

We connect our industrial-grade negative air machine to your main duct — creating a powerful vacuum that captures all loosened debris and prevents any contamination from entering your living spaces.

3
Rotary Brush Agitation

High-powered rotary brushes are inserted through every register, scrubbing the interior walls of your ducts and dislodging years of compacted dust, debris, and buildup.

4
Complete Extraction

All loosened contaminants are fully extracted by our vacuum system into sealed HEPA-filtered collection bags — nothing escapes back into your home.

5
Sanitization Treatment

We apply an EPA-approved antimicrobial sanitizer throughout your ductwork to eliminate bacteria, mold spores, and odors — leaving your system fresh and hygienic.

6
Final Inspection & Report

We perform a post-cleaning camera inspection so you can see the results with your own eyes, and provide you with a full written report of the work completed.

Ready to Breathe Cleaner?

Get a free, no-obligation quote for your air duct cleaning. We serve all of Long Island, NYC, New Jersey, and Connecticut. Most jobs completed same day or next day.

📞 (347) 930-3719
Service 02
Chimney Service
Sweep · Inspection · Repair · CSIA Certified

Your chimney is a critical safety system in your home — yet it's one of the most overlooked. Every time you use your fireplace or heating appliance, creosote, soot, and debris accumulate inside the flue. Left uncleaned, creosote is highly flammable and is the leading cause of chimney fires across the United States.

BHM Services USA provides comprehensive chimney sweeping, detailed inspections, and professional repair services. Our CSIA-certified technicians go beyond the surface — we inspect every component of your chimney system to ensure it's operating safely and efficiently, season after season.

BHM Services USA — Professional Chimney Repair
Why Chimney Service Is Critical
🔥
Prevent Chimney Fires

Creosote — the black, tar-like byproduct of burning wood — is extremely flammable. Even a thin layer can ignite and cause a devastating chimney fire. The NFPA recommends annual cleaning and inspection.

☠️
Prevent Carbon Monoxide Poisoning

A blocked or damaged chimney can cause deadly carbon monoxide to back up into your home. This odorless gas kills hundreds of Americans every year — a clean, clear chimney is your first line of defense.

🏗️
Protect Structural Integrity

Moisture, freeze-thaw cycles, and acid from combustion gases slowly deteriorate mortar and brick. Early detection and repair prevents expensive structural damage and keeps your chimney standing strong.

💨
Optimal Draft & Efficiency

A clean, properly functioning chimney draws smoke efficiently. Obstructions like bird nests, debris, or soot buildup restrict airflow, causing smoke to back up into your home and reducing heating efficiency.

🏠
Home Insurance Compliance

Many homeowner insurance policies require annual chimney inspections. Our detailed written reports satisfy insurance requirements and protect your coverage in the event of a claim.

💎
Protect Your Home's Value

A well-maintained chimney is a selling point. Buyers and inspectors look closely at chimney condition. Annual service keeps your chimney in premium condition and protects your property value.

Warning Signs Your Chimney Needs Service
Smoke entering the room when using the fireplace
Strong odor coming from the fireplace (especially in summer)
Black, oily stains on fireplace walls or damper
Difficulty starting or maintaining a fire
Visible cracks in mortar joints or brickwork
White staining (efflorescence) on exterior chimney
Animal sounds or nests inside chimney
More than 1 year since last inspection or cleaning
Our Chimney Process
1
Level I / II Inspection

We perform a thorough inspection of all accessible areas — firebox, damper, smoke chamber, flue liner, and exterior crown and cap — identifying any damage, deterioration, or obstructions.

2
Professional Sweeping

Using certified chimney brushes and a high-powered dual-motor vacuum system with HEPA filtration, we sweep the entire flue from top to bottom — removing all creosote, soot, and debris with zero mess in your home.

3
Firebox & Smoke Chamber Cleaning

We meticulously clean the firebox interior, smoke shelf, and smoke chamber — areas often overlooked by less thorough companies but critical for safe, efficient fireplace operation.

4
Obstruction Removal

Bird nests, leaves, and other debris blocking the flue are completely removed. We also inspect and clean the chimney cap and spark arrestor to ensure proper function.

5
Repairs & Waterproofing (if needed)

Cracked mortar joints, damaged crowns, missing caps, and deteriorated liners are repaired or replaced. We also apply professional-grade waterproofing sealant to protect against moisture intrusion.

6
Detailed Written Report

Every job concludes with a comprehensive written inspection report documenting the condition of your chimney, work performed, and any recommendations — satisfying insurance and real estate requirements.

Protect Your Home & Family

Don't wait until there's a problem. Annual chimney service is the most important safety investment you can make for your home. Call us today — most appointments available within 24 hours.

📞 (347) 930-3719
Service 03
Dryer Vent Cleaning
Fire Prevention · Efficiency Restoration · Safety Certified

According to the U.S. Fire Administration, nearly 3,000 dryer fires occur every year, causing over $35 million in property damage and dozens of deaths. The cause in the vast majority of cases? Failure to clean the dryer vent.

Lint is one of the most combustible household materials — it's even used as fire starter by campers. When it accumulates in your dryer vent, it creates a fire waiting to happen. BHM Services USA removes 100% of the lint and blockage from your entire vent system, from the dryer connection all the way to the exterior exhaust — protecting your home and family.

Why Dryer Vent Cleaning Saves Lives
🔥
Eliminates Fire Hazard

Lint is extremely flammable. A clogged vent traps heat and creates the perfect conditions for a dryer fire. Regular cleaning is the single most effective way to prevent this deadly hazard in your home.

☠️
Prevents CO Poisoning

Gas dryers with blocked vents can cause carbon monoxide to back up into your home. CO is odorless and colorless — you won't know it's happening until it's too late. A clear vent is essential safety.

⚡
Cuts Drying Time in Half

A clogged vent forces your dryer to run multiple cycles to dry a single load. After cleaning, most customers report clothes drying in one cycle — saving hours of time and significant electricity costs every month.

💰
Reduces Energy Costs

The U.S. Department of Energy estimates a clogged dryer vent can increase energy consumption by up to 30%. Cleaning your vent pays for itself quickly in reduced utility bills.

🔧
Extends Dryer Lifespan

Overheating caused by poor ventilation is the #1 cause of premature dryer failure. A clean vent keeps your dryer running at optimal temperature — adding years of life to your appliance.

👕
Protects Your Clothes

Excess heat from a blocked vent degrades fabric fibers over time. Your clothes wear out faster and may develop scorching or shrinkage. Clean vents mean gentler, more effective drying.

Danger Signs — Act Immediately
Clothes take more than one cycle to dry
Dryer or clothes are extremely hot to the touch
Burning smell when dryer is running
Laundry room feels unusually hot and humid
Lint visible around the dryer or outside vent hood
Outside vent flap doesn't open when dryer runs
It's been more than 1 year since last cleaning
Dryer shuts off before clothes are dry
Our Dryer Vent Process
1
Full System Evaluation

We assess the entire dryer vent system — checking vent length, bends, material type, and the condition of the exterior exhaust hood. We identify any code violations, crushing, or damage before we begin.

2
Disconnect & Access

We safely disconnect the dryer from the vent, giving us full access to the entire duct run from the appliance connection to the exterior exhaust — including any bends, elbows, or transitions in the system.

3
Rotary Brush Cleaning

Using flexible rotary brush systems and high-powered air pressure, we scrub every inch of the interior vent walls — dislodging compacted lint, debris, and any bird nests or blockages.

4
Complete Extraction

All loosened lint and debris are fully extracted using our industrial vacuum — nothing is left behind and nothing escapes into your laundry room or home.

5
Exterior Hood Cleaning & Inspection

We clean and inspect the exterior vent hood and flap — ensuring it opens and closes properly, has no damage or obstructions, and is properly sealed against weather, birds, and pests.

6
Airflow Verification Test

After cleaning, we perform an airflow test to confirm your vent is clear and functioning optimally. You'll notice the difference immediately — faster drying times and a cooler, safer laundry room.

Don't Risk a Dryer Fire

Dryer vent cleaning is the fastest, most affordable safety upgrade you can make for your home. Most jobs take under 60 minutes. Call now — same-day appointments often available across Long Island and NYC.
